# Function to strip diacritics, tatweel, unify hamza?
import regex as re

def normalize_arabic(s):
    if not s:
        return ""
    # Remove diacritics
    s2 = re.sub(r'[\p{Mn}]', '', s)  # remove combining marks
    # Remove tatweel
    s2 = s2.replace('ـ', '')
    # Normalize hamza forms
    trans = str.maketrans({
        'أ':'ا','إ':'ا','آ':'ا','ؤ':'و','ئ':'ي','ى':'ي','ة':'ه',
        'ٰ':'', # dagger alif
    })
    s2 = s2.translate(trans)
    # remove punctuation and non-letters digits
    s2 = re.sub(r'[^\p{Arabic}\s]', ' ', s2)
    # collapse spaces
    s2 = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s2).strip()
    return s2

# Build normalized texts
quran_norm = [normalize_arabic(q['ayah_text']) for q in quran]
# Search for target normalized
target_norm = normalize_arabic("وَعَدَدَ لَكُمْ فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ")

from difflib import SequenceMatcher

def similarity(a,b):
    return SequenceMatcher(None, a, b).ratio()

def token_f1(a,b):
    ta = set(a.split())
    tb = set(b.split())
    if not ta or not tb: return 0.0
    inter = len(ta & tb)
    prec = inter / len(ta)
    rec = inter / len(tb)
    if prec+rec == 0: return 0.0
    return 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)

target_n = normalize_arabic("وَعَدَدَ لَكُمْ فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ")
best_q = None
best_ratio = 0.0
best_tf1 = 0.0
for i,q in enumerate(quran):
    s = quran_norm[i]
    r = similarity(target_n, s)
    tf1 = token_f1(target_n, s)
    if r > best_ratio:
        best_ratio = r; best_q = (q, s, r, tf1)
best_ratio, best_tf1, best_q[0]['ayah_text'][:100], best_q[1][:100]
